Monday Pre-Market Roundup:

bitcoin:native Bitcoin slides down below $62,000, down over 3% as sentiment turns bearish (again).

Crude oil slips as OPEC+ raises output targets for fifth straight month.

US stock futures rise as inflation fears ease post-payrolls report.

SK Hynix launches massive $28-$29 Billion Nasdaq listing to capitalize on AI boom.

Japanese Yen hovers near 40-year lows as Goldman Sachs turns more bearish.

7.6.26

GEOPOLITICS, ENERGY & TRADE
- OPEC+ & Strait of Hormuz: Seven OPEC+ nations, led by Saudi Arabia and Russia, agreed to roll back curbs and add 188,000 bpd in August. Major Persian Gulf producers are rapidly ramping output, with tanker traffic out of Hormuz entering a "new normal" of 30 to 60 vessels a day—enough to ease global supply pressures and push WTI lower.
- SPR Replenishment: Rebuilding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve to pre-war levels will take 15 to 18 months even at an optimistic buying rate of 200,000 barrels a day. 
- USMCA Sunset: The Trump administration is expected to formally declare on Wednesday that it will not extend the USMCA, triggering a sunset clause that starts a decade-long clock to wind down the North American free trade zone.
- Agricultural Heat Wave: US soybean and corn futures surged over 3% as a scorching "heat dome" hit major growing regions right as corn enters its vulnerable pollination stage. Heat waves have also reportedly damaged a third of the French corn crop.
- NATO Summit: President Trump meets with Ukrainian President Zelenskyy at Wednesday's NATO summit in Turkey, an event charged with tensions over European defense spending and the Iran war.

MACROECONOMICS, RATES & FX
- FOMC Minutes & Services Data: Wednesday's release of the June FOMC minutes (Chair Kevin Warsh's debut) will be scrutinized for hawkish rate trajectories, as futures imply a 64% chance of a September rate hike. Today, markets focus on the ISM Services index following a dominant services job print from ADP.
- Intervention Watch (Yen): The options market is sending a bearish signal on the yen, with one-year sentiment favoring the dollar for the first time in three years. Goldman Sachs revised its USD/JPY forecast up to 165, noting Japanese authorities are staying on the sidelines.
- South Korean Pension Flows: National Pension Service Chairman Kim Sung-joo dismissed fears of a 74 trillion won domestic equity "selling bomb," stating the chance of a massive market-disrupting selloff is "zero" even as the fund's rebalancing waiver expires.

ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE, SEMIS & TECH
- Samsung Q2 & Memory Hikes: Samsung reports its Q2 update tonight, with analysts expecting an 18-fold profit surge (barring outsized strike-averting employee bonus provisions). The company is aggressively pushing to hike Q3 DRAM and LPDDR average selling prices by 20%.
- SK Hynix Mega-Listing: SK Hynix launched its $28.1 billion U.S. share sale roadshow. Final pricing is set for Thursday, with the ADRs debuting Friday in what will be the second-largest share sale in history behind SpaceX. It is expected to join the SOX index, paving the way for massive passive inflows.
- SpaceX NDX Inclusion: SpaceX (SPCX) will be added to the Nasdaq 100 after today's close, which JPMorgan estimates will draw $4.3 billion in passive inflows. The underwriter quiet period also expires tomorrow, unleashing fresh analyst coverage.
- Nvidia (NVDA) Delay: Asian tech stocks slumped after research firm SemiAnalysis reported that Nvidia's next-generation Kyber NVL144 AI server rack system is delayed by more than a year due to printed circuit board manufacturing setbacks.
- Sun Valley Summit: The 2026 Allen & Co. conference begins Tuesday in Idaho, with top AI executives including OpenAI's Sam Altman and Anthropic's Dario Amodei on the guest list.
- Hedge Fund Selling & Rotation: Short selling drove a third straight week of net equity sales by global hedge funds. Morgan Stanley warns momentum is fading in semiconductor stocks as investors rotate toward laggards and hyperscalers.

DOMESTIC POLITICS & POLICY
- Progressive Primary Upsets: Democratic socialists scored major upsets in Colorado, including Melat Kiros unseating 29-year incumbent Rep. Diana DeGette. Combined with recent wins in NY and Maine, progressive candidates are successfully disrupting the establishment status quo.
- Student Loan Sticker Shock: New Department of Education repayment plans went into effect on July 1, featuring stricter borrowing caps for graduate students and parents. Millions of borrowers face massive sticker shock as they transition into new plans this summer.

CALENDAR
- Mon: US ISM Services, S&P Global US Services PMI. BoC Business Outlook. Fed's Waller speaks in Rome. SpaceX added to Nasdaq 100 (after close).
- Tue: 2026 Sun Valley Conference kicks off. Japan 30Y Bond Auction, Labor Cash Earnings. US Trade Balance, JOLTS Job Openings. API Oil Data.
- Wed: USMCA non-extension formal declaration. FOMC Meeting Minutes (2:00 p.m. ET). US MBA Mortgages, Wholesale Inventories. DOE Oil Inventories. 10-Year Note Auction. Trump meets Zelenskyy at NATO summit (Turkey).
- Thu: China CPI/PPI. US Weekly Jobless Claims, Existing Home Sales. Fed's Williams, Fed's Logan speak. SK Hynix final IPO pricing.
- Fri: SK Hynix begins US trading. Japan PPI. German CPI. Canadian Employment. CFTC COT, Fed H8 Report.

MARKET UPDATE — MONDAY, JULY 6, 2026

Markets are starting the week with a strange mix:

🔸 tech futures are bouncing
🔸 oil is falling
🔸 bond yields remain stubborn
🔸 and the Japanese yen is flashing risk warnings again

This is not a simple “risk-on” day.
It is a market trying to celebrate lower inflation pressure while quietly asking whether the AI trade has become too crowded.

🟢 MARKETS: TECH LEADS THE REBOUND

US futures are higher after the July 4 holiday weekend.

S&P 500 futures: around +0.4%
Nasdaq 100 futures: around +1.2%
Dow futures: slightly negative / broadly flat
VIX: around 16.4

The Nasdaq is leading because chip stocks are rebounding after last week’s sharp sell-off.

Intel and Micron rose premarket after heavy losses in semiconductors last Thursday.

Simple explanation:
When chip stocks rise, the Nasdaq usually rises because AI and semiconductor companies are now some of the biggest engines of the US stock market.

But this rebound needs confirmation.
The semiconductor sector has already had a huge run this year, and investors are now asking whether future profits can justify the prices.

🟢 BONDS & FED: THE MARKET STILL FEARS HIGHER RATES

Treasury yields are slightly lower this morning:

1-year yield: around 3.94%
2-year yield: around 4.11%
10-year yield: around 4.46%
30-year yield: around 4.97%

The key point is this:
Yields are not collapsing, even though oil has fallen and jobs data recently weakened.
That matters.

Simple explanation:

Bond yields are like the interest rate the US government has to pay to borrow money.

When yields stay high, it tells us investors still think inflation or tight Fed policy may remain a problem.

The market is now waiting for Wednesday’s Fed minutes from the first meeting under Chair Kevin Warsh.

Traders want to know whether the Fed is preparing to stay tough on inflation — or whether weaker jobs and cheaper oil will reduce pressure for another rate hike.

This week also brings a 10-year Treasury auction on Wednesday and a 30-year auction on Thursday.

That matters because the stock market is trying to rally while bond yields remain stubbornly high.

The market is now waiting for Wednesday’s Fed minutes from the first meeting under Chair Kevin Warsh.

Traders want to know whether the Fed is preparing to stay tough on inflation — or whether weaker jobs and cheaper oil will reduce pressure for another rate hike.

This week also brings a 10-year Treasury auction on Wednesday and a 30-year auction on Thursday.

That matters because the stock market is trying to rally while bond yields remain stubbornly high.

Traders are also watching today’s final S&P Global services and composite PMI readings.

Forecasts point to continued expansion, with the services PMI expected at 51.4 and the composite PMI expected at 52.2.

Simple explanation:

A PMI reading above 50 usually means business activity is still growing.

🟢 OIL: FROM HORMUZ PANIC TO SUPPLY GLUT FEARS

Oil continues to fall.

WTI crude: around $68–69
Brent crude: around $71–72

OPEC+ has agreed to raise production by 188,000 barrels per day in August.

That is the fifth consecutive monthly increase.

This comes as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has improved after the US-Iran 60-day ceasefire.

Weekly crude oil tanker crossings have rebounded to about 57 (up from a wartime average of 15). However, this is still significantly lower than pre-war baseline, which was was roughly 230 to 240 crude oil tanker crossings per week.

Simple explanation:

A few months ago, markets feared the world might not get enough oil.

Now the worry is moving in the opposite direction: too much supply, weaker demand, and lower prices.

That is why some banks now see Brent potentially falling towards $60–65 by year-end if Hormuz keeps reopening and supply continues to return.

For ordinary people, cheaper oil can mean lower petrol and transport costs.

For markets, cheaper oil can reduce inflation pressure.
For energy stocks, however, lower oil can hurt profits.

🟢 AI & TECH: A HUGE WEEK FOR THE AI TRADE

This week could test investor appetite for AI again.

Three major events matter:

🔸Samsung is expected to give a second-quarter update tomorrow.

🔸SK Hynix is preparing an approximately $28bn US listing, one of the largest share sales in history.

🔸SpaceX officially joins the Nasdaq-100 tomorrow.

South Korea is also becoming a warning sign for AI-related speculation.

The KOSPI has surged dramatically over the past year, but volatility has been extreme and foreign investors have been pulling money out.

That matters because Samsung and SK Hynix are now central to the global AI supply chain.

SpaceX’s inclusion matters because funds tracking the Nasdaq-100 may need to buy the stock automatically.

Simple explanation:

When a company joins a major index, some funds have to buy it because their job is to copy that index.

That can create short-term demand.

But it does not guarantee long-term gains.

The real question is still whether earnings, margins and cash flows can support today’s valuations.

🟢 THE EARNINGS BUBBLE QUESTION

The next major test is earnings season.

Investors will be watching whether AI spending by big technology companies is producing enough revenue growth to justify today’s valuations.

In simple terms:

The market has already priced in a lot of good news. Now companies need to prove the profits are real.

This is the biggest quiet risk beneath the surface.

Wall Street analysts now expect very strong S&P 500 profit growth, helped by AI, chips and resilient consumers.

But some investors worry expectations have risen too fast.

The danger is not that AI is fake.

The danger is that markets may be pricing AI profits as if everything goes perfectly.

Simple explanation:

Imagine a student who is expected to score 99% in every exam.

Even if they score 90%, that may still be excellent — but markets may treat it as disappointment.

That is the risk with AI stocks now.

Great companies can still fall if expectations become unrealistic.

🟢 SMALL CAPS: A CONTRARIAN SIGNAL

The Russell 2000 has had a strong year, but investor flows into small-cap funds remain low by historical standards.

That is interesting because neglected areas of the market can sometimes become attractive when too much attention is focussed on mega-cap tech.

Simple explanation:

If everyone is crowded into the same few giant companies, smaller companies can be ignored.

Sometimes that creates opportunity.

But small caps usually need falling rates, stable growth and easier credit conditions to perform well.

🟢 CURRENCIES: THE YEN WARNING RETURNS

The dollar is stronger today.

The yen remains weak, with Goldman Sachs reportedly raising its 12-month USD/JPY forecast to 165.

This matters because a weak yen can encourage the carry trade.

That means investors borrow cheaply in yen and invest in higher-returning global assets.

Simple explanation:

It is like borrowing from a friend who charges almost no interest, then using that money to chase bigger returns elsewhere.

It feels easy while the yen stays weak.

But if the yen suddenly strengthens, traders may have to unwind those positions quickly.

That can force selling across stocks, bonds and other risk assets.

Japan may intervene again if the yen weakens too far, but intervention usually gives temporary relief unless the interest-rate gap changes.

🟢 CORPORATE DEAL FLOW

Corporate activity is also picking up.

🔸Comcast’s Sky is buying ITV’s TV business in a deal reported around the 🔸–$2bn range.

🔸EasyJet shares rose after a takeover approach from Castlelake.

These are not the main market stories, but they show that dealmaking is still alive despite high rates and uncertain growth.

🟢 STOCKS TO WATCH TODAY

Several individual stocks are also in focus:

🔸 Intel and Micron — rising premarket as chip stocks try to rebound after last week’s sharp sell-off.

🔸 easyJet — jumped in London after a takeover approach from Castlelake.

🔸 Comcast / ITV — Comcast’s Sky is buying ITV’s TV business in a deal reported around $1.6bn–$2bn.

Simple explanation:

These are not the main market driver, but they show where traders may see extra movement today.

🟢 WHAT IT MEANS

Today’s market is being pulled in two directions.

Bullish side:

🔸 Oil is falling
🔸 Inflation pressure may ease
🔸 Tech is rebounding
🔸 AI demand remains powerful
🔸 Earnings expectations are strong

Bearish side:

🔸 Bond yields remain high
🔸 Fed minutes could sound hawkish
🔸 AI expectations may be too stretched
🔸 The yen carry trade is a hidden risk
🔸 Oil’s fall could also signal weaker demand

The market is fragile.

The main risk is that investors are assuming three things at once:

🔸inflation falls

🔸earnings surge

🔸AI keeps delivering

If all three happen, the rally can continue.

If one fails, volatility may return quickly.

Thought for the Day:
“The market does not punish optimism. It punishes optimism without a margin of safety.”

Sources: 
MarketWatch, WSJ, Barron’s, Reuters, Financial Times, Bloomberg, Goldman Sachs, TradingView

WEEKLY MACRO MARKET PULSE: Global markets are experiencing a significant shift in risk appetite, driven by June's US non‑farm payrolls report, which showed an addition of just 57,000 jobs against forecasts of 110,000. The disappointing data cooled expectations for near-term Federal Reserve rate hikes, weakening the US dollar and sparking a relief rally across non‑yielding precious metals. 

Spot gold rebounded past $4,170 per ounce before easing to around $4,148, while silver eased to nearly $62 after trading near $63.50 earlier. Analysts highlight silver’s next breakout targets in the $64–72 zone. Major Wall Street banks project year‑end gold targets between $4,500 and $5,200, with @Citi setting the lower bound and @jpmorgan cutting its short‑term forecast to $4,500 amid tactical pauses. 

After hitting an intraday record above $5,500 earlier this year before correcting in late June, gold continues to stand out as one of the strongest assets of the past year, underpinned by geopolitical risks and stronger Asian market demand, according to data from the @GOLDCOUNCIL.

Meanwhile, oil prices remain under pressure, with Brent crude sliding below $72 a barrel after OPEC+ announced its fifth consecutive monthly output boost of 188,000 barrels per day for August, coinciding with easing geopolitical tensions as Strait of Hormuz shipping routes recover. 

In digital assets, Bitcoin recovered steadily from a July 2 low near $57,750, holding firm in the $62,596–63,999 range, while Ethereum bounced to nearly $1,773 from its June 8 low of $1,505 despite lighter holiday trading volumes. 📊👀

7 月 7 日 A 股早盘完整观点解读

外围全线上涨 + 三星业绩炸裂

         昨晚美股、中概集体收红，叠加三星电子二季度利润同比暴涨 19 倍、存储周期逻辑再强化，先理清外围利好与隐藏压制风险，再拆解今日大盘节奏、板块分层策略与核心风险。

一、大盘整体节奏：小幅高开震荡，仅情绪修复、非趋势反转

1、开盘驱动：美股三大指数、中概金龙指数全线上涨，费城半导体收涨，叠加三星超预期存储业绩，今日 A 股小幅高开是大概率；
2、三是港股存在大额解禁抛压，北向资金日内分歧加大，高位科技股容易出现高开兑现；三重压制反弹高度的利空，决定反弹空间有限：一是美国财政部流出报告草案，直接警示 AI 赛道存在泡沫风险，类比 2000 年互联网泡沫，压制全球科技股估值预期；二是韩国股市情绪脆弱，三星业绩公布后韩股高开跳水，存储板块冲高回落，拖累全球半导体情绪；
3、操作纪律：今日冲高缩量就是减仓窗口，不开新仓、不追加仓位。当前行情只是外围利好带来的脉冲式情绪修复，不存在单边反转基础；
4、真正趋势拐点需等待 7 月中旬关键数据与会议：7 月 17/21 日国内 GDP 经济数据落地，叠加重磅政策会议定调，在此之前市场整体维持箱体震荡格局。

二、板块强弱分层：执行杠铃配置策略，进攻短线逢高兑现、防御做底仓对冲

（一）进攻短线主线（仅博弈日内修复，不追高，冲高分批减仓）

存储芯片（今日核心主线，催化最密集）
         覆盖标的：江波龙、兆易创新、佰维存储、深科技，同时延伸 HBM 先进封装长电科技、盛合晶微、配套材料华海诚科。
         多重利好共振：三星二季度利润暴涨核心驱动力是 HBM、DRAM 涨价；SK 海力士启动 280 亿美元美股 ADR 上市；隔夜美股存储板块全线走高；国内 SSD 终端产品价格翻倍上涨，行业供需缺口至少延续至 2027 年。

半导体设备、算力硬件

《7月6日全球财经前瞻：资产逻辑重构与交易机制变革》

全球资产定价逻辑正经历从宏观叙事向业绩与成本精细化管理的切换。美联储7月维持利率不变的概率约为77%，市场高度依赖美国6月服务业PMI及ISM非制造业PMI数据来校验通胀韧性与短端利率路径。黄金与白银价格受避险情绪与利率边际变化驱动，现货黄金触及4200美元/盎司，短期波动率加剧；原油则因8月产油国拟日均增产18.8万桶的供给侧扰动，维持在68.54美元/桶附近的宽幅震荡。宏观层面，全球央行处于通胀回落与政策正常化的磨合期，欧元区投资者信心及零售数据成为观测区域经济韧性的关键窗口。

AI硬件产业链正式从概念炒作期过渡至业绩验证期，三星电子二季度业绩预期及亚洲AI供应链出口数据成为全球科技定价的锚点。尽管硬件链景气度处于上行周期，但随着涨幅扩大，后续资本开支兑现、订单可见度与毛利率表现将替代情绪驱动成为股价核心支撑。与此同时，消费电子领域传来动态，折叠iPhone预期于2026年下半年问世，组装出货量约700万至800万部，带动高端结构件与精密制造链的预期修正。金融科技领域，大模型应用边界正进入严格的合规制度评估期，行业逻辑从技术扩张转向合规安全。

A股市场交易新规于今日正式落地，沪市主板及ETF盘后固定价格交易机制的扩展，显著优化了配置型资金的调仓效率与执行平稳度。数据统计显示，截至6月30日，ETF两融余额增至1160.88亿元，融资资金在半导体、通信等成长主题与黄金ETF防御属性之间构建了双向配置策略。此外，ST板块交易限制的调整标志着监管对存量博弈风险控制的强化。未来市场主线将由单一消息驱动转向“资源股盈利弹性 + 科技链订单确定性 + 制度红利带来的流动性优化”的综合逻辑，投资者需关注杠杆水平与宏观数据再定价带来的溢出效应。

【关键词】：#美联储 #FOMC #服务业PMI #ISM非制造业PMI #全球供应链压力指数 #黄金价格 #原油供给 #AI硬件链 #存储芯片 #三星电子 #光模块 #工业富联 #中际旭创 #新易盛 #A股交易新规 #ETF配置 #两融余额 #半导体 #消费电子 #折叠iPhone #金融科技 #大模型合规 #天问二号 #深空探测 #资源股 #高股息 #资产配置 #波动率 #宏观政策 #利率预期

【观点】：本报告呈现出当前全球资本市场从“宽松预期博弈”向“底层资产与业绩兑现双重回归”的深刻转型。

从激进视角看，市场已进入“去伪存真”的强监管与强验证窗口。AI硬件产业链虽然账面数据靓丽，但7月财报季将成为“分水岭”，缺乏实质订单转化与毛利支撑的标的将面临估值修正，这不仅是财务表现的波动，更是资本市场对AI技术落地深度的终极拷问。保守视角则认为，当前黄金站上4200美元及油价的低开，反映出全球大类资产在防御与波动之间的极度拉扯，地缘政治与宏观不确定性正在降低风险资产的容错率。

中立视角的分析则指出，本次A股交易新规的落地是市场基础设施建设的关键一步，通过引入盘后固定价格交易，实际上是在对冲散户情绪波动，引导资金向ETF等工具型产品流动，这对于市场的长期稳定性至关重要。

核心风险在于“相关性误导”。市场常将宏观降息预期与科技股上涨强相关，但在当前滞涨风险与高利率常态化的双重挤压下，这种因果链条极其脆弱。盲目追高黄金或AI概念，实质上是在忽略“业绩验证窗口”的系统性风险。建议投资者应剥离情绪化表达，聚焦于：1. AI产业链中具备高壁垒、高毛利、确定性资本开支的龙头；2. 具有稳定分红且具备成本护城河的能源与资源品种；3. 随着制度优化，利用ETF工具进行流动性管理和杠杆控制。

当前市场最大的盲点在于，多数投资者过分关注“叙事”，而忽略了“成本”。无论是半导体制造的边际成本，还是金融AI应用的高额合规成本，都将显著削薄未来几年的净利润率。将逻辑抽象至高维，这本质上是全球市场从资本扩张周期转向效率存量周期的缩影。决策的核心应从“交易趋势”转向“验证护城河”，避免在机构博弈的震荡中成为流动性的牺牲品。

The “global glut” argument mistakes a corridor between the Gulf and China for the whole market.
Then treats the price fall it causes as confirmation.
Strategic reserve drawdowns and clearing of Gulf’s trapped backlog are keeping prices down.
Both are one-off and both are draining.

UAE and Iraq heavily discounted trapped oil to clear the backlog. China’s teapots bought it cheap. Iran didn’t match the discount, keeping its oil in floating storage instead.

Global surplus should broaden across grades and regions, and eventually ease product markets too.
But it isn’t.
Visible in West African crude.
And in record crack spreads.

Gulf output remains a quarter below Feb levels, Hormuz transit still well below norms, and US crude inventories near multi-decade lows.

This is a race – drawdowns and backlog running out, against normal production and transit flows returning to replace them.
The market is pricing this as settled. It isn’t.
Unpredictable friction in the Strait could see props disappear before real flows are back.

The third batch of 2026 "Two Weights" construction projects has been allocated, with over 193.5 billion yuan in ultra-long-term special treasury bonds earmarked. With this allocation, the full list of "Two Weights" projects for this year has now been finalized. Since the beginning of the year, the National Development and Reform Commission, in coordination with relevant industry authorities, has allocated 800 billion yuan to support 1,417 major projects.

The State Council's Office of Work Safety has launched a nationwide campaign to crack down on illegal and non-compliant safety practices, targeting key sectors including mining, chemicals, and fire safety. Authorities are required to severely punish violations such as falsified safety certifications, malicious illegal acts, forced violations, and reckless operations.

Affected by Typhoon Meisak, heavy rainfall has persisted across multiple regions in Guangxi, leading to emergency conditions at several reservoirs in Nanning. The National Flood Control and Drought Relief Headquarters has upgraded its flood emergency response to Level II for Guangxi. China Railway Guangzhou Group has suspended some train services from July 6 to 8. The central government has allocated 100 million yuan in budgetary investment to support emergency recovery efforts in Guangxi.

China Equities Markets

A-shares edged lower on reduced volume. The SSE Index closed down 0.06% at 4,041.24, the Shenzhen Component Index fell 1.16%, and the ChiNext Index dropped 1.77%. The Wind All China Index declined 0.84%, with market turnover reaching 3.11 trillion yuan. Computing hardware and semiconductor supply chain stocks suffered heavy losses, while commercial aerospace and rare earth sectors weakened. Coal, pork, and poultry stocks rose, alongside gains in banking and synthetic diamond segments.

Hong Kong equities extended their rebound. The Hang Seng Index closed up 1.14% at 23,616.32, the Hang Seng Tech Index rose 0.94%, and the Hang Seng China Enterprises Index gained 1.46%. Coal, consumer, and biopharmaceutical stocks led gains, while large model and CPO-related stocks plunged, and hardware and semiconductor sectors declined. Net inflows via the Stock Connect program exceeded HK$20.5 billion, with Tencent Holdings receiving net purchases of nearly HK$2.5 billion.

The Hong Kong government proposed amendments to the Securities Market Listing Rules, allowing the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission to withdraw previously issued objections to listing applicants. The proposal also suggests introducing less disruptive alternatives to trading suspensions, enabling the SFC to impose post-listing conditions on issuers under appropriate circumstances.

Kuaishou announced that Tencent Holdings sold approximately 273 million Class B shares via an off-market block trade on July 6. After the transaction, Tencent's stake in Kuaishou will decline from 15.68% to 9.37%, and it will no longer be considered a major shareholder.

According to Shanghai Stock Exchange records, Yushu Technology's STAR Market IPO registration became effective on July 2. Yushu Technology is a leading developer of high-performance general-purpose robots. The company plans to raise 4.202 billion yuan through the offering, primarily for R&D of intelligent robot models.

China Industry News

The Central Cyberspace Administration has completed the first phase of its special campaign to rectify AI application abuses, removing over 14,000 non-compliant websites, apps, and AI agents, and clearing more than 6 million illegal or unlawful content items. The next phase will focus on combating AI-generated disinformation and online bot networks.

According to OpenRouter data, global AI model query volume reached 46.7 trillion tokens last week, with China's AI models accounting for 23.45 trillion tokens — a 15% weekly increase. This marks the sixth consecutive week of growth and the tenth consecutive week of global leadership. In the same period, U.S. AI model queries totaled 4.28 trillion tokens.

The 2026 World Robotics Conference will be held from August 19 to 23. In 2025, China accounted for 90% of global humanoid robot shipments, with over 330 models launched. From January to May this year, revenue from China's robotics industry surpassed 90 billion yuan, up 26.9% year-over-year.

According to Hangzhou Beike Research Institute, Hangzhou's ten districts recorded 7,711 second-hand home transactions in June, up about 5% year-over-year. First-half cumulative transactions reached 464,780 units, down approximately 5%. First-half new home sales totaled 16,000 units, a 27% year-over-year decline — the lowest level in 15 years — while average prices rose 18% year-over-year.

Pork prices have surged sharply, returning above the 10 yuan per kg threshold for the first time in four months. According to China Pig Network data, the national average price for external three-breed live pigs rose from 9.47 yuan per kg on June 26 to 11.06 yuan per kg on July 6 — a 1.59 yuan increase over 10 consecutive days, representing a 16.8% gain.

Fintech

The UK's Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) warned that regulators are entering an "arms race" to keep pace with the widespread adoption of AI in financial services. The FCA report highlighted that AI's "highly personalized" nature may foster algorithmic bias, opaque pricing, and targeted manipulative behavior.

Corporate News & Updates

Intel confirmed price increases for select consumer and server CPUs, citing shifting market conditions, rising costs, and sustained demand. Consumer flagship products will see price hikes of $30 to $50, while data center products will rise by hundreds to over a thousand dollars.

Sources indicate that Huawei's Mate 90 series, slated for release this fall, will feature a new Kirin chip based on the Taolü architecture.

Broadcom announced an agreement with Apple to extend their partnership through 2031, supplying custom ASIC chips for multiple generations of Apple products.

Leaked information suggests Google's Gemini 3.5 Pro will launch on July 17, featuring a significant leap in front-end and visual code generation capabilities. It reportedly outperformed Anthropic's Fable 5 in several benchmarks but still lags behind in complex reasoning and engineering tasks.

Tencent's Hunyuan officially released Hy3. Building on the preview version, Hy3 improved post-training data quality and diversity, expanded RL compute scale, and delivered significant gains in inference, agent capabilities, and long-context handling, achieving performance comparable to larger global flagship models.

Microsoft's Xbox division has launched a strategic restructuring plan, including approximately 3,200 layoffs — 20% of its workforce — and the divestment of five game development studios. Additional cuts of about 3,200 positions are planned across other Microsoft sales departments, bringing total layoffs to approximately 6,400.

Porsche plans to cut an additional 4,000 jobs at its core Stuttgart plant, bringing the total impact of this and previous rounds to nearly 8,000 positions. The company is also considering reducing production capacity at its Weissach R&D center by up to 30%.

Global Macro & Policy

U.S. President Trump said resolving the Ukraine conflict will be "much faster than people think." He added that Russian President Putin wants to end the war "very strongly," and Ukrainian President Zelensky "actually wants to end it now."

Federal Reserve Governor Waller stated that forward guidance remains a valuable tool but must be used flexibly to avoid constraining policy options. He supports the Fed's 2% inflation target and noted that labor market stabilization allows monetary policy to focus more on inflation.

South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol chaired a public-private meeting on semiconductors, physical AI, and AI data centers, urging a "speed race" on the three major projects. He emphasized the government must proactively remove barriers to corporate investment and project implementation, and must not delay investment or project delivery due to bureaucratic procedures.

The first Global AI Governance Dialogue opened in Geneva, Switzerland. United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres warned that the rapid evolution of AI technology has outpaced global regulatory responses, and urged the international community to urgently establish unified global rules to mitigate potential risks.

The U.S. Institute for Supply Management reported that U.S. services PMI fell to 54 in June from 54.5 the prior month, slightly below expectations of 54.2. The employment index posted its largest monthly gain since 2024 and returned to expansion, while the prices paid index fell to a four-month low. Business activity and new orders growth softened.

Global Equity Markets

U.S. equities closed higher. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.29% to 53,055.91, the S&P 500 gained 0.72% to 7,537.43, and the Nasdaq Composite advanced 1.12% to 26,121.16. Boeing and IBM led the Dow with gains over 3%. The Wind US Magnificent Seven Index rose 1.42%, with Tesla up over 6% and Meta up nearly 3%. SpaceX fell nearly 1%. The Nasdaq China Golden Dragon Index rose 1.71%, with iQIYI up nearly 6% and BOSS Direct up over 5%.

European markets ended mixed. The German DAX rose 0.15% to 25,817.89; the French CAC 40 fell 0.33% to 8,479.87; and the UK FTSE 100 declined 0.26% to 10,651.77. Utilities and healthcare stocks led losses, while financials and banks held up relatively well, as markets paused after four consecutive weeks of gains.

South Korea's KOSPI rose initially by nearly 3% before closing down 0.46% at 8,051.33. Japan's Nikkei 225 edged down 0.01% to 69,737.69, dragged lower by AI-related stocks like Kioxia. India's S&P BSE Sensex closed up 0.67% at 78,285.07.

SK Hynix officially launched its U.S. IPO, planning to issue 177.9 million American Depositary Shares (ADS), representing 17.79 million ordinary shares. Net proceeds are expected to total approximately $28 billion, down slightly from the previously anticipated $29 billion. Anchor investors have expressed interest in purchasing up to $7 billion in ADS. The final offering price will be set on Thursday, with trading expected to begin on Friday on the Nasdaq.

South Korean lawmaker Ahn Cheol-soo called on social media for "strong corrective measures," including delisting, against leveraged ETFs tracking Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. He claimed the KOSPI has become "a casino" and labeled such products "a complete policy failure."

Sources indicate India's securities regulator, SEBI, plans to significantly relax short-selling restrictions, nearly doubling the number of stocks eligible for securities lending and lowering margin requirements for stock borrowing.

SpaceX will be officially added to the Nasdaq-100 Index on July 7, just 15 days after its Nasdaq debut on June 12 — the fastest inclusion in the index's history.

Bonds

China's interbank bond market remained buoyant, with yields on major government bonds generally declining, especially in medium- to long-term tenors, though movements were mostly under 1 basis point. Treasury futures rose, with the 30-year contract up 0.23%. Liquidity remained ample, as the People's Bank of China conducted 7 billion yuan in 7-day reverse repurchase operations and 1 trillion yuan in 3-month outright reverse repurchase operations.

Concerns over Japan's deteriorating fiscal condition pushed Japanese government bond yields higher. The yield on newly issued 10-year JGBs briefly rose to 2.830%, the highest since October 1996. The 20-year yield climbed 4.5 basis points to 3.8%, and the 30-year yield rose 5 basis points to 4.075%.

U.S. Treasury yields mostly declined. The 2-year yield fell 1.66 basis points to 4.108%, the 3-year yield dropped 1.71 basis points to 4.133%, the 5-year yield fell 1.40 basis points to 4.200%, the 10-year yield dipped 0.20 basis points to 4.467%, and the 30-year yield rose 1.51 basis points to 4.984%.

Commodities

Precious metals futures generally declined. COMEX gold fell 0.26% to $4,176.30 per ounce, and COMEX silver dropped 0.53% to $62.48 per ounce. A softer Fed stance on forward guidance, coupled with weaker-than-expected nonfarm payrolls, reduced near-term rate hike expectations. While long-term gold demand remains supportive, short-term speculative pressure weighed on prices.

U.S. crude oil futures fell 0.26% to $68.60 per barrel; Brent crude declined 0.17% to $72.00 per barrel. OPEC+ approved its fifth consecutive month of output increases, while shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz eased, reducing geopolitical risk premiums. Saudi Aramco slashed its official crude oil prices for Asia by the largest margin since 2000 to gain market share, while global crude demand growth expectations moderated. With no major strategic stockpiling underway, sustained supply surplus continues to pressure prices.

Most base metals on the LME rose. LME zinc gained 1.07% to $3,579 per tonne, LME tin rose 0.90% to $53,100 per tonne, LME aluminum climbed 0.73% to $3,113 per tonne, LME nickel advanced 0.46% to $16,500 per tonne, LME copper rose 0.33% to $13,411 per tonne, and LME lead fell 0.56% to $1,881 per tonne.

South Korean prosecutors have indicted four domestic oil refiners and four employees from two of them for colluding to fix fuel prices, estimating damages at approximately $17 billion. President Yoon Suk Yeol has demanded strict crackdowns on fuel price collusion to curb inflation.

Saudi Arabia cut its official selling price for Arab light crude delivered to Asia in August by $11 per barrel, at a $1.50 discount to regional benchmarks — the largest discount in at least 26 years and the first discount since the 2020 price war.

India's Minister of Petroleum and Natural Gas stated that India is expanding domestic crude oil exploration to increase domestic supply and reduce import dependence. India's domestic crude output in FY2025 was 25.98 million tonnes, equivalent to approximately 522,000 barrels per day — meeting only about 10% of national demand.

FX & Currency Markets

On Monday, the onshore CNY/USD rate closed at 6.7925 at 16:30, down 121 basis points from the previous close. The offshore rate closed at 6.7924. The PBOC's official mid-rate was set at 6.8066, a 19-basis-point depreciation from the prior day.

The U.S. Dollar Index held steady at 100.87. Non-U.S. currencies traded mixed. EUR/USD rose 0.04% to 1.1440, GBP/USD gained 0.30% to 1.3389, AUD/USD rose 0.24% to 0.6956, USD/JPY climbed 0.45% to 162.0965, USD/CAD rose 0.04% to 1.4206, USD/CHF increased 0.20% to 0.8052, and the offshore CNY/USD fell 87 basis points to 6.7945.

South Korea's foreign exchange market has launched a 24-hour trading system. Under the new mechanism, KRW/USD trading runs continuously from 6 a.m. Monday to 6 a.m. Saturday, excluding weekends and January 1. All other statutory holidays remain active trading days. Finance Minister Koo Jun-hyeok stated that round-the-clock forex trading marks the beginning of the won's globalization.

Midday Briefing (July 7, 2026, 11:40 Beijing Time)

Core Summary

The primary theme in the Asia-Pacific session has shifted from "strong AI hardware earnings" to "profit-taking at highs." Despite Samsung reporting a 19-fold profit increase, South Korean markets and chip stocks plummeted, signaling that AI trade overcrowding has become the dominant concern. While U.S. pre-market trading continues to carry over the overnight tech rebound, domestic Chinese (A-share/H-share) and broader Asia-Pacific markets are increasingly focused on high valuations, IPO lock-up expirations, and the risks of rebounding oil prices.

Tech / AI & Compute / Semiconductors & Memory / Optical Communications

Major News: Samsung reported an estimated Q2 operating profit increase of 19x year-on-year to 89.4 trillion KRW, with revenue estimated at 171 trillion KRW. However, its stock fell by approximately 7.9% in morning trading, suggesting that the AI memory boom has already been fully priced in. (Reuters)

Investment Opportunities: HBM, DRAM, and NAND maintain price support; FT reports DRAM prices rose 44% quarter-over-quarter, with NAND up 53%. (Financial Times)

Risk Warning: Simultaneous declines in Samsung, SK Hynix, and Kioxia indicate that the market is beginning to question the sustainability of AI capital expenditures and potential oversupply. (Financial Times)
U.S. Markets (Pre-market)
Major News: Overnight tech gains in the U.S. have provided a positive mapping for the pre-market session, with the S&P 500 up 0.72%, Nasdaq up 1.12%, and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index up 2.2%. (Reuters)

Corporate Updates: Broadcom rose 3.7% following the extension of its custom chip partnership with Apple until 2031; Microsoft announced 4,800 layoffs. (Reuters)

Industry Data: SOXX last traded at $581.51 (+2.58%); QQQ at $722.82 (+1.36%).
A-Share Market (Morning Summary)
Major News: No verifiable new major policy signals during this period.

Investment Opportunities: Samsung’s profit figures and rising memory prices continue to act as a catalyst for A-share memory, semiconductor equipment, PCB/CCL, and AI server supply chains.

Risk Warning: The high-level pullback in South Korean chip stocks may dampen sentiment and suppress chasing behavior in the A-share memory sector. (Reuters)
Hong Kong Market (Morning Summary)
Major News: Pressure from Hong Kong IPO lock-up expirations is mounting. A record volume of shares is set to be released this week, including 25.6 million shares of AI developer Knowledge Atlas, accounting for nearly 6% of its circulating shares. (Reuters)

Investment Opportunities: U.S. tech rebounds map onto HK tech stocks, but sustainability depends on the support from Southbound capital.

Risk Warning: The supply of newly unlocked shares in AI and tech stocks may suppress risk appetite in the Hong Kong market. (Reuters)
Asia-Pacific Markets (Morning Summary)
Major News: Asia-Pacific stocks retreated, led by South Korea. The MSCI Asia Pacific (excluding Japan) index fell 0.73%, KOSPI dropped 4.1%, and the Nikkei fell 1.08%, directly triggered by profit-taking in AI chips. (Reuters)

Investment Opportunities: If the correction continues, investors may shift from chasing growth to screening for performance in South Korean HBM, Taiwanese AI manufacturing, and Japanese equipment chains.

Risk Warning: The Japanese Yen remains near a 40-year low; currency intervention risks continue to disturb Asia-Pacific assets. (Reuters)
Cryptocurrency (BTC, ETH, DOGE, etc.)
Major News: BTC remains in a range above $63,000 (approx. $63,280); ETH is approx. $1,624.95; DOGE is approx. $0.075185.

Risk Warning: DOGE fell approx. 2.83% during the day, highlighting uneven sentiment recovery.

Industry Data: BTC intraday range: $61,350—$64,435.
Macroeconomics
Major News: Oil prices saw a mild rebound but remain suppressed by recovering supply. Reuters reports that the market is simultaneously digesting Middle East risks and OPEC+ production increases. (Reuters)

Investment Opportunities: If oil prices remain low, the cost side for aviation, logistics, chemicals, and Asian energy-importing economies continues to benefit.

Risk Warning: If the oil price rebound is driven by geopolitical risk rather than improved demand, the certainty of "low oil price" trades will be compressed.
Geopolitics
Major News: Continued uncertainty regarding Iran is suppressing risk appetite. Reuters reports that investors remain focused on the potential for escalating U.S.-Iran tensions and the subsequent impact on energy and markets. (Reuters)

Risk Warning: The Strait of Hormuz, energy transport, and sanctions waivers remain tail risks for oil prices.

This briefing is based on public information and is for reference only; it does not constitute investment advice.

💥Broadcom, Apple 칩 계약 2031년까지 연장

💡핵심 동력：AI 반도체 공급망에 다시 자금이 붙을 명분. Apple도 외부 커스텀 칩 없이는 어렵다는 뜻이다.

🎯체크포인트：Nasdaq·SOXX가 단기 추세 유지하면 AI beta 생존. AAPL만 강하고 NVDA/AVGO가 못 따라가면 방어적 순환.

＿＿＿＿

💥Microsoft 4,800명 감원, AI 투자 회수 압박

💡핵심 동력：시장은 이제 “AI에 얼마 쓰나”가 아니라 “언제 돈 버나”를 본다. Mag 7 밸류에이션에 직접 압박.

🎯체크포인트：MSFT 약세 + US10Y 상승이면 고밸류 성장주 위험. SPX가 버티면 자금은 소프트웨어보다 반도체로 이동.

＿＿＿＿

💥ISM 서비스업 54.0, 경기와 물가 둘 다 살아있다

💡핵심 동력：고용은 확장 복귀, 가격지수는 67.7로 여전히 높다. Fed 완화 기대를 주기엔 애매하고, 위험자산엔 찝찝하다.

🎯체크포인트：US10Y 4.5% 돌파 시 BTC·금·성장주 압박. DXY 100.5 하회가 리스크온 조건.

＿＿＿＿

💥Samsung 이익 19배, 그래도 주가는 급락

💡핵심 동력：좋은 실적도 이미 가격에 반영됐으면 매도 재료다. AI 반도체는 이제 “좋다”가 아니라 “예상보다 훨씬 좋다”가 필요하다.

🎯체크포인트：KOSPI와 SOXX 동반 약세면 AI 전면 랠리 기대 금지. NVDA 단기 추세 이탈 시 BTC 심리도 흔들린다.

＿＿＿＿

💥유가 반등 제한: OPEC+ 증산, 사우디 아시아 가격 인하

💡핵심 동력：전쟁 프리미엄은 빠지고 있지만 미·이란 리스크는 남아있다. 원유는 Fed 인플레 경로의 스위치다.

🎯체크포인트：Brent 73 돌파 시 인플레 트레이드 재점화. WTI 68 하회 시 Fed 매파 압력 완화.

＿＿＿＿

💥금은 Fed 대기 속 약세, 홍콩은 금 청산 인프라 강화

💡핵심 동력：단기적으로는 달러와 금리가 금을 누른다. 하지만 아시아 금 시장 인프라 확대는 중장기 유동성 재평가 신호다.

🎯체크포인트：Gold 4,130 지지 + DXY 약세면 4,170 반등. US10Y 4.5% 돌파면 금·BTC 롱 모두 짧게.

＿＿＿＿

📊 딩딩 롱숏 레이더：
Crypto [변동성 속 약강세] | 미국주식 [분열형 강세] | Gold [중립 약세] | Oil [변동성 약강세]

💡 오늘 장 성격：[분열형 Risk-on]

🎯 핵심 레벨:
BTC 63.4k / US10Y 4.50% / Brent 73

오늘 밤 핵심은 BTC 양봉이 아니다. ETF 유입이 미 국채금리 반격을 버틸 수 있느냐다. BTC가 63.4k를 회복할까, 아니면 또 한 번 유동성 함정일까? 댓글로 관점 남겨줘. 이 스레드는 공유해라. 초록 캔들 하나 보고 뛰어드는 사람은 너무 많다.

text:
📍 BTC x AI Chips — DingDing Cross-Market Notes (2026/07/07)

Core take: Money is back. Conviction isn’t.

🎯 Playbook: Keep only strong spot bags. Don’t chase perps. Wait for BTC >63.4k or <62k.

One-liner: ETF inflows are not a bull-market decree. Below 63.4k, every pump is suspect.

＿＿＿＿

💥BTC ETF inflows flip positive: +$224M, BTC near 63k

💡Core driver: After six days of outflows, institutions are testing the water again. But this smells more like short-covering plus softer macro than real conviction.

🎯Tape: BTC above 63.4k + another ETF inflow day = 65k in play. Lose 62k and this bounce is just a cleaner-looking dead cat.

＿＿＿＿

💥Broadcom locks Apple chip deal through 2031

💡Core driver: AI-chip plumbing gets another bid. Apple still needs external custom silicon; AVGO stays in the “picks and shovels” lane.

🎯Tape: If Nasdaq and SOXX hold the 5D trend, AI beta survives. If AAPL pops while NVDA/AVGO lag, that’s defensive rotation, not risk-on.

＿＿＿＿

💥Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs. AI capex gets questioned.

💡Core driver: The market is done worshipping AI spend blindly. Mag 7 now has to answer: when does capex turn into cash flow?

🎯Tape: MSFT breaking lower + US10Y rising = multiple compression. If SPX holds, money rotates to semis, not software.

＿＿＿＿

💥ISM Services at 54.0: economy alive, inflation alive too

💡Core driver: Employment back in expansion, prices still hot at 67.7. Not weak enough for Fed relief, not clean enough for risk assets to party.

🎯Tape: US10Y >4.5% pressures BTC, gold and high-multiple tech. DXY <100.5 is what risk bulls need.

＿＿＿＿

💥Samsung prints 19x profit. Stock still gets sold.

💡Core driver: Classic market cruelty. If good news is priced, earnings become exit liquidity. AI chips need “beat and raise,” not just “beat.”

🎯Tape: KOSPI + SOXX rolling over means no broad AI rescue. If NVDA loses short-term trend, crypto risk appetite gets clipped too.

＿＿＿＿

💥Oil rebound capped: OPEC+ adds supply, Saudi cuts Asia prices

💡Core driver: War premium is being stripped out, but US-Iran tail risk is not dead. Oil is now the Fed inflation switch.

🎯Tape: Brent >73 brings inflation trades back. WTI <68 cools Fed-hawk pressure.

＿＿＿＿

💥Gold slips before Fed minutes; HK boosts gold clearing

💡Core driver: Rates and USD are capping gold short term. But Asia building gold market infrastructure is not noise.

🎯Tape: Gold holds 4,130 + DXY fades = 4,170 bounce. US10Y >4.5% means don’t overstay gold or BTC longs.

＿＿＿＿

Crypto [Choppy Bullish] | Stocks [Split Bullish] | Gold [Neutral-Bearish] | Oil [Choppy Bullish]

💡 Session type: [Split Risk-on]

BTC 63.4k / US10Y 4.50% / Brent 73
